assertions.description: Initial state shows two panels: left with true orthogonal projection (locked), right with adjustable decomposition at the optimal position
Dragging the teal point along the subspace changes the decomposition
Left panel always shows minimum residual length; right panel shows current (potentially longer) residual
Moving away from perpendicular increases residual length
Student discovers that only the perpendicular decomposition minimizes residual length — perpendicularity is what makes projection optimal
assertions.verification: Any deviation from perpendicular increases the residual length shown in the right panel
